reasoning: Response A has the most genuinely distinct set of five: Candlewake (tending a zero-g combustion sphere) and Slosh (herding a water blob held only by surface tension) both abandon the ball/body/trajectory frame entirely, while Tetherball Cascade, The Long Drift, and Spinwright each occupy a separate conceptual space (recoil-web, single perpetual trajectory, angular-momentum matching) with vivid, internally coherent mechanics. C is well-crafted and bold where it counts (Centrifuge restoring weight via spin plus Coriolis, Passant making balance a passable team resource), but it clusters: Tetherball Deep and Holdfast both hinge on footing/momentum and its Long Arc duplicates B's Broken Parabola. B maps cleanly to five mechanics but stays inside the modify-a-ball-game frame and is thinly developed, so it ranks last despite reasonable spread.
